queries.note: per-call subprocess incl. graph parse; in-process MCP serves from cache

discoveredAndFixed: catastrophic regex backtracking in shingles.tokenize on unterminated-quote content — an input-dependent HANG (overlap sat at 100% CPU >15 min on one 2-node group); replaced with a linear-time matcher + regression test

quadratic twin seeding through hub labels and unbounded same-name pairwise confirmation — bounded by declared caps (hub >50 callers, 200k pair budget, 12-node group samples, 400-line body shingles), all reported in overlap.md

shardsDecision.rationale: 16k symbols load monolithically as a 13.5MB graph with ~200ms subprocess queries and a 4s memoized re-map; 10x this scale still fits memory comfortably. The answer-preserving sharding contract was never wired to any CLI/MCP surface and the measured need does not exist; git history preserves the implementation if a 100k+-symbol case ever materializes.

findingsSpotCheck: top findings are real compiler duplication (substituteExpression x9 drifted, transformConstructorBodyWorker x3 at 80% body) — 46 ready merges, ~1757 LOC, gate-green
